summary: postinstall script exfiltrates AWS IMDS credentials to attacker webhook
details: hello244a@1.0.41 ships a package.json whose postinstall lifecycle script queries the AWS EC2 instance metadata service (IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/) and POSTs the retrieved IAM role credentials to a hardcoded Pipedream webhook at https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?real_aws_keys=1. The query string self-labels the payload as 'real_aws_keys=1'. The tarball contains only package.json with no source, README, dependencies, or declared functionality — the package's sole purpose is to run this credential-theft payload automatically on npm install. Any developer or CI/build host with reachable IMDS (EC2, ECS, container platforms) will have its IAM role credentials silently sent to the attacker on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
